summary: Descriptive overview of dutyofcare in substantive law: foreseeability, special relationships, no-duty rules.
key_points: foreseeability is a fundamental concept in tort law.
Legal standards for dutyofcare are established through case law and statutory interpretation.
Courts apply specific tests to determine dutyofcare in litigation.
The doctrine of dutyofcare has evolved through Supreme Court precedents.
Understanding dutyofcare requires analysis of both historical context and modern applications.
philosophy_guard_statement: This fragment describes existing legal doctrines and their applications without advocating for normative changes.
